revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Akatsuchiyama Kofun]][[ja:赤土山古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | name = Akatsuchiyama Kofun | native_name =赤土山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Akatsuchiyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Akatsuchiyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Akatsuchiyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.61947222222222|N|135.83958333333334|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|qid=Q502196|lt=Tenri, Nara|1=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th- 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|37|10.1|N|135|50|22.52|E|qq=}}|qq=}}|qq=}} {{nihongo|'''Akatsuchiyama Kofun'''|赤土山古墳||qq=}} is a [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=keyhole-shaped Kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, located in the Ichinomoto-cho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|qid=Q502196|lt=Tenri, Nara|1=Tenri, Nara}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1992.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163411|title=赤土山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024|qq=}}</ref> It is one of the tumuli that make up the Tōdaijiyama Kofun Group, which consists of five keyhole-shaped tombs and 40 to 50 circular and square tombs that date mainly from the early to middleKofun period. ==Overview== The Akatsuchiyama Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located in the southeastern part of the Nara Basin, in a hilly area called Tōdaijiyama, which stretches from east-to-west. The south side of the mound has collapsed due to a landslide, and the front end has been leveled As a result of a total of nine [[archaeological excavation]]s conducted since 1987, it was found to be a keyhole-shaped tumulus with a stepped structure and a remaining mound length of 103.5 meters, with ''[[fukiishi]]'' covering the entire exterior of the tumulus, and an estimated total original length of 110 meters. There is no moat around the mound, but the front end is divided by a trench. The posterior circular portion is approximately 44 by 33 meters, with rounded corners and a projection at the tip, giving it a unique shape compared to a typical ''zenpō-kōen-fun''. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is unclear as it is thought to have been destroyed by a landslide, but it is assumed that it contained a clay coffin, as excavations of the sedimentary soil on the south side of the rear mound have revealed clay coffin fragments, vermilion, stone bracelets, and stone replicas as {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}}. Other grave goods include {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|qid=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} pottery and ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' clay figures, the latter of which is mainly cylindrical ''haniwa'' that surround the mound, as well as figurative ''haniwa'' such as house-shaped, lid-shaped, and chicken-shaped ''haniwa''. Some rows of cylindrical ''haniwa'' still standing have been discovered during excavations. Based on excavated remains, this tumulus was built in the early Kofun period and is thought to be the grave of a member of the ancient powerful {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|qid=Q11417873|lt=Wani clan|1=Wani clan}}.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404|qq=}}{{in lang|ja|qq=}}</ref> <gallery> Akatsuchiyama Kofun, saishi-ikou.jpg|Reproductions of house-shaped ''haniwa'' from the Akatsuchiyama Kofun]] </gallery> The site was developed into a park in 2009, and is about a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ichinomoto Station|en|Ichinomoto Station|qid=Q3936059|lt=Ichinomoto Station|1=Ichinomoto Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|en|Sakurai Line|qid=Q865801|lt=Sakurai Line|1=Sakurai Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/> ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|qid=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== {{template-inline|Akatsuchiyama Kofun|qq=}} *[https://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1397015205268.html Tenri City home page]{{in lang|ja|qq=}} *[https://www.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/ikasu-nara/bunkashigen/main10613.html Nara Prefecture History and Culture Database]{{in lang|ja|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
[[en:Akatsuchiyama Kofun]]
